creative_brief: Clean, minimal design with a hero image of cattle on pasture. Emphasis on the 18-month timeline with visual contrast against industrial alternatives.
subject: Meet Rachel Ward and her cattle in Kempsey
preheader: One farm. One farmer. This is where your beef comes from.
body:
Hi {first_name},

Rachel Ward runs a regenerative cattle farm in Kempsey, on the northern NSW coast. Her herd is 100% grass-fed. No hormones. No feedlots. No grain finishing. Just pasture, patience, and land that gets healthier every year.

Rachel knows every paddock her cattle graze. She rotates them across the property so the soil recovers between grazing cycles. It is slow work. It takes longer than industrial farming. But the result is beef with a depth of flavour you can actually taste.

Most supermarket beef has spent weeks in cold storage by the time it reaches you. Rachel's beef reaches our Brookvale hub in days. The difference shows up the moment you cook it. Richer colour. Better texture. A roast that reminds you what beef is supposed to taste like.

This is what FarmThru is. A farm-direct grocery store where every product traces to a named farm. Zero middlemen. You order online. You collect from Brookvale, Monday to Friday. No commitment.

This week, Rachel's beef is available at the hub.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
primary_text: Rachel Ward runs a regenerative cattle farm in Kempsey. Her herd is 100% grass-fed. This is where your beef comes from.
headline: Meet Rachel Ward and her cattle in Kempsey
